plan_metadata.name: Sample Hello World Meeting Local Debug

steps.description: Type 'my first meeting app' into the sample search input field within the "Samples" tab of Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.

Click the "Create" button for the "My First Meeting App" sample in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension panel within Visual Studio Code.
And input ${{var:app_name:hello-world-in-meeting}} into the run time variable.
Click the browser tab's close button ('X') in the Microsoft Teams web app interface to close the current Teams chat session.
Enter 'https://localhost:53000/index.html#/tab' into the address bar of a new tab in Google Chrome.
Press Enter in the Chrome address bar to navigate to "https://localhost:53000/index.html#/tab".
Click the "Advanced" button on the Chrome privacy error page for https://localhost:53000, located below the warning message and marked by a red crosshair.
Click the "Proceed to localhost (unsafe)" link on Chrome's "Your connection is not private" warning page to bypass the certificate error and access the localhost site.
Click the "Microsoft Teams Tab" browser tab in Google Chrome to activate it.
Click the "Allow" button in the Microsoft Teams browser popup requesting permission to look for and connect to devices on your local network in the Google Chrome interface.
Click the "Home" tab in the chat header of the Microsoft Teams web app interface to open the in-meeting app sample view.

Click the "Reload tab" option in the tab actions dropdown menu within the Microsoft Teams web interface. The dropdown is accessed from the "Home" tab of a channel labeled "testing" under "Favorites" in the Chat section. The precise interaction point is highlighted with a red crosshair over the "Reload tab" menu item for clear identification.
Click the "Home" tab in the Microsoft Teams web interface, located in the top navigation bar of the "testing" chat workspace.
Click the "Reload tab" option in the dropdown menu under the "Home" tab in the Microsoft Teams web interface, highlighted by a red crosshair.
Click the "Home" tab in the top navigation bar of the Microsoft Teams web application within the "testing" chat interface.

Click the "Remove" menu item (with a trash can icon) in the tab context menu within the Microsoft Teams web application interface, to remove the current tab from the "Home" section of the "testing" chat.
Click the "Remove" button in the confirmation dialog to permanently delete the Home tab within the Microsoft Teams web application (teams.microsoft.com) on the "testing" chat interface.
